# Error Translator

> Because Errors Have Feelings Too

## Overview

Error Translator is a client-side React web application that humorously translates programming error messages into empathetic, human-readable descriptions with therapeutic advice. It features a retro-futuristic neon CRT aesthetic.

## Tech Stack

- React 18 with TypeScript (strict mode)
- Vite 5 (bundler and dev server)
- Tailwind CSS 3 (styling with CSS custom properties)
- Lucide React (icons)
- ESLint 9 (linting)

## Architecture

The app is entirely client-side with no backend. Error translations come from a hardcoded database of 18 entries in `src/data/errors.ts`.

### Core Flow

User input → substring matching against error database → simulated processing animation → display result (emoji + human translation + therapy advice)

### Key Files

- `src/App.tsx` — Root component
- `src/hooks/useTranslation.ts` — State machine: idle → translating → done
- `src/data/errors.ts` — Error database, suggestion list, processing log messages
- `src/components/` — UI components (ErrorInput, Header, ResultCard, TranslationLogs, IdleState, ScanlineOverlay, Footer)
- `src/index.css` — Tailwind directives, CSS variables (neon theme), keyframe animations

### Error Matching

Case-insensitive substring search. Unmatched errors receive a random translation from the database.

## Commands

- `npm run dev` — Start dev server (port 5173)
- `npm run build` — Production build
- `npm run lint` — Run ESLint
- `npm run typecheck` — TypeScript type checking
- `npm run preview` — Preview production build

## Design

- Color scheme: Neon pink (#ff0066) on black
- Font: JetBrains Mono (monospace)
- Effects: CRT scanlines, glitching title text, pulsing glow animations
- Responsive typography using CSS clamp()

## Repository

- Repo: arnoldwender/error-translator
- No backend or external API dependencies
- Supabase JS is installed but unused (placeholder for potential future use)
